text: In summary, this study leverages the powerful inherent capabilities of EVs and sophisticated genetic engineering to construct a novel "killing two birds with one stone" therapeutic platform for combating acute pancreatitis. By transfecting specific cells with specially designed plasmids, we engineered these cells to secrete EVs containing the Z domain, enabling the precise conjugation of anti-Prdx1 antibody to the surface, while simultaneously encapsulating the therapeutic small molecule quercetin within their lumen. Our comprehensive research demonstrates that these engineered EVs possess elastase-responsive properties, ensuring targeted delivery and functional regulation within the pathological pancreatic microenvironment. This groundbreaking approach simultaneously achieves dual therapeutic effects: on the one hand, the timely extracellular release of anti-Prdx 1 antibody effectively inhibits macrophage-mediated inflammation by neutralizing extracellular Prdx1; on the other hand, the subsequent intracellular delivery of quercetin powerfully ameliorates acinar cell damage by enhancing intracellular antioxidant defenses. This innovative, multi-mechanism strategy not only achieved breakthroughs in solving the specific problem of SAP, but more importantly, it created a versatile and adaptable general tool that lays the foundation for developing precise and personalized treatments for various complex diseases in the future.
